summary: Matched `lbHeap_80015DF8` by adding the original extra `p->size` vararg to a final `OSReport` call in `src/melee/lb/lbheap.c`. The format string only consumes `p->size / 1024`, but retaining the unused-looking extra argument reproduced target codegen and moved the function from 99.87654% to 100.0% with size still 324/324 bytes.

key_files.reason: One-line change in `lbHeap_80015DF8`: `OSReport(" / %5d KB\n", p->size / 1024)` became `OSReport(" / %5d KB\n", p->size / 1024, p->size)`.

what_changed: Added `p->size` as an extra argument to the final `OSReport` call inside the heap report loop in `lbHeap_80015DF8`.
No functional formatting change is implied by the format string, which only consumes the divided KB value.
The change matched the target function exactly: `lbHeap_80015DF8` improved from 99.87654% to 100.0%, while function size remained 324/324 bytes.
smart_moves: Recognized that an apparently unused vararg can still be required for matching because it affects the generated call sequence/register setup.
Kept the fix minimal and localized to the mismatching `OSReport` call instead of restructuring the report function.
Documented why the extra argument exists so future cleanup does not remove it as redundant.
decomp_lessons: For vararg functions such as `OSReport`, do not assume every passed argument is consumed by the format string in the retail binary.
Unused-looking varargs can be intentional matching artifacts; preserving them may be necessary for byte-accurate codegen.
When matching report/debug-style functions, compare the call-site argument list against target codegen, not only against semantic C formatting behavior.
A 100% match can depend on retaining semantically redundant source details.
naming_conventions: The PR retained existing decomp names and fields: `lbHeap_80015DF8`, `lbHeap_80431FA0`, `arena_hi`, `arena_lo`, and `p->size`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added an unconsumed vararg to `OSReport` to reproduce target codegen.
Confirmed the function remained 324/324 bytes after the change.
Ran `DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH=/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/usr/lib cargo run -p melee-issues`; reported issues were existing elsewhere and not from `src/melee/lb/lbheap.c`.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body explicitly explains that the extra `p->size` vararg is needed to match target codegen despite the format string not consuming it.
follow_up_queries: Find other `OSReport` calls where the retail binary may pass extra unused varargs.
Search for report/debug functions stuck near 100% where call-site argument count differs from the apparent format string.
Audit future cleanup PRs for accidental removal of semantically redundant varargs needed for matching.
